warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: The Long Island Rail Road finished 2024 with increased ridership, continuing the upward trends of recent years. Total ridership was 75.5 million customers.

payload.raw_value_text: an increase of 1.7% percentage points from 2023
scheduled trains increasing from 303,519 in 2023 to 316,954 in 2024
M9 fleet, with an MDBF of 434,481 miles
combined diesel fleet achieved an MDBF of 46,211 miles
about 217,000 customers were tallied each weekday
Off-peak weekday ridership, on the other hand, at 107,910

issues: metric_claim payload with raw_value_text should include normalized numeric value or value_min/value_max

payload.raw_value_text: 2024 Fall overall LIRR satisfaction increased to 76%

payload.description: MTA Long Island Rail Road celebrated its 190th Anniversary on April 24th, 2024
The opening of Grand Central Madison in 2023 altered the operation in an unprecedented way

tool_args.label: GCM vs Penn AM Peak Split

payload.raw_value_text: AM Peak ridership stabilized at a 60/40 split between Penn and GCM
payload.scope: Grand Central Madison vs Penn Station

payload.raw_value_text: ridership has currently reached 86% of 2019's ridership in the first quarter of 2025
The best total February OTP in the LIRR recorded history with 96.41%

payload.project_name: Construction of the new Yaphank station
payload.description: Construction of the new Yaphank station and rehabilitation work at Mastic-Shirley station
Resulted in increased cleaning and enhanced customer experience
Continued to realize significant ridership increases, up about 20% from 2023 (which was over 50% higher than pre-COVID ridership)

payload.claim_text: the LIRR remained the busiest commuter railroad in North America

tool_args.label: West Hempstead Branch Midday Ridership Increase

payload.raw_value_text: 343% increase in average midday weekday off-peak ridership – from 230 in 2019 to 1,020 in 2024
